member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.voxMeshShell(PicoGK.Mesh,System.Single)
member.summary: Creates a shelled (hollow) Voxels object from a mesh. The mesh object is offset in all directions by the radius. The mesh doesn't have to be closed (it could consist of just one triangle)
member.returns: Voxels object

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.voxCombineAll(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{PicoGK.Voxels}@)
member.summary: Create a new Voxels object using the global library instance, and adds all voxel fields in the container, returning the result
member.returns: All voxel fields combined

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.voxFromVdbFile(System.String)
member.summary: Create Voxels from a OpenVDB file (.vdb) using the global library instance. This function searches for the first compatible field in the .vdb file (OpenVDB supports storing many fields of various types in one file). PicoGK Voxels need an openvdb::GRID_LEVEL_SET field. Incompatible fields and other compatible fields after the first one are ignored. For more sophisticated .vdb file handling, use PicoGK.OpenVdbFile Note: When you save your own VdbFiles with multiple fields in it you cannot rely on the fields being in the same order as you saved them. In other words, if you use PicoGK.OpenVdbFile to save multiple Voxel fields to a file, loading Voxels with voxFromVdbFile will load any one, not necessarily the one you saved first.
member.param: Path and file name of the VDB file
member.returns: A voxel field, based on the .vdb data
member.exception: If file is empty or no compatible field found, an exception is thrown.

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.Offset(System.Single)
member.summary: Offsets the voxel field by the specified distance. The surface of the voxel field is moved outward or inward Outward is positive, inward is negative
member.param: The distance to move the surface outward (positive) or inward (negative) in millimeters

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.voxOffset(System.Single)
member.summary: Offsets a copy of the voxel field by the specified distance. The surface of the voxel field is moved outward or inward Outward is positive, inward is negative.
member.param: The distance to move the surface outward (positive) or inward (negative) in millimeters
member.returns: Resulting field

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.DoubleOffset(System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Offsets the voxel field twice, by the specified distances Outwards is positive, inwards is negative

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.voxDoubleOffset(System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Offsets a copy of the voxel field twice, by the specified distances Outwards is positive, inwards is negative
member.returns: Returns the resulting field

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.TripleOffset(System.Single)
member.summary: Offsets the voxel field three times by the specified distance. First it offsets inwards by the specified distance Then it offsets twice the distance outwards Then it offsets the distance inwards again This is useful to smoothen a voxel field. By offsetting inwards you eliminate all convex detail below a certain threshold by offsetting outwards, you eliminated concave detail below a threshold by offsetting inwards again, you are back to the size of the object that you started with, but without the detail Usually call this with a positive number, although you can reverse the operations by using a negative number

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.voxTripleOffset(System.Single)
member.summary: Offsets a copy of the voxel field three times by the specified distance. First it offsets inwards by the specified distance Then it offsets twice the distance outwards Then it offsets the distance inwards again This is useful to smoothen a voxel field. By offsetting inwards you eliminate all convex detail below a certain threshold by offsetting outwards, you eliminated concave detail below a threshold by offsetting inwards again, you are back to the size of the object that you started with, but without the detail Usually call this with a positive number, although you can reverse the operations by using a negative number
member.returns: Returns the resulting field

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.Smoothen(System.Single)
member.summary: Same as TripleOffset
member.returns: Returns the resulting field

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.voxSmoothen(System.Single)
member.summary: Same as TripleOffset
member.returns: Returns the resulting field

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.OverOffset(System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Similar to DoubleOffset, but allows you to specify the offsetted distance to the original surface as the second parameter. The surface is first offset by fFirstOffsetMM Then the surface is offset so that the final offset from the surface is fFinalSurfaceDistInMM

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.voxOverOffset(System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary:
Similar to DoubleOffset, but allows you to specify the offsetted distance to the original surface as the second parameter. The surface is first offset by fFirstOffsetMM Then the surface is offset so that the final offset from the surface is fFinalSurfaceDistInMM.
If not specified, the surface is where it was before.
This function is used to eliminate detail in the voxel field. It generates fillet-like results when used with a positive first offset.
member.param: Absolute final offset from initial surface

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.Fillet(System.Single)
member.summary: Creates a fillet-like effect. Same as OverOffset with second value 0 Since the effect is similar to a fillet, this makes a lot of code more readable

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.voxFillet(System.Single)
member.summary: Creates a fillet-like effect. Same as OverOffset with second value 0 Since the effect is similar to a fillet, this makes a lot of code more readable

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.voxShell(System.Single)
member.summary:
Creates a shell of a voxel field. The wall thickness is the size of the offset.
If a positive offset is supplied, the wall is outside the object, i.e. the void inside the shell has the shape and dimensions of the current object.
If the offset is negative, the object's dimensions remain the same, but a void is created that is created by negatively offsetting the object.

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.voxShell(System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Creates a shell of a voxel field, by offsetting and subtracting copies of the field. One of the offsets can be zero, but if both are zero, an empty voxel field is the result
member.param: Offset to be used to create the void
Offset to be used to create the outer shell
Optional smoothing parameter that allows you to smoothen the internal void

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.RenderMesh(PicoGK.Mesh@)
member.summary: Renders a mesh into the voxel field, combining it with the existing content
member.param: The mesh to render (needs to be a closed surface)

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.RenderImplicit(PicoGK.IImplicit@,PicoGK.BBox3@)
member.summary: Render an implicit signed distance function into the voxels overwriting the existing content with the voxels where the implicit function returns smaller or equal to 0 You will often want to use IntersectImplicit instead
member.param: Implicit object with signed distance function
Bounding box in which to render the implicit

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.IntersectImplicit(PicoGK.IImplicit@)
member.summary: Render an implicit signed distance function into the voxels but using the existing voxels as a mask. If the voxel field contains a voxel at a given position, the voxel will be set to true if the signed distance function returns smaller or equal to 0 and false if the signed distance function returns > 0 So a voxel field, containting a filled sphere, will contain a Gyroid Sphere, if used with a Gyroid implict
member.param: Implicit object with signed distance function

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.voxIntersectImplicit(PicoGK.IImplicit@)
member.summary: Same as IntersectImplicit, but uses a copy of the current voxel field and returns the result.

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.RenderLattice(PicoGK.Lattice@)
member.summary: Renders a lattice into the voxel field, combining it with the existing content

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.ProjectZSlice(System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Projects the slices at the start Z position upwards or downwards, until it reaches the end Z position.

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.voxProjectZSlice(System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Makes a copy of the voxel field and applies the ProjectZSlice function to the copy.

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.bIsEqual(PicoGK.Voxels@)
member.summary: Returns true if the voxel fields contain the same content

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.CalculateProperties(System.Single@,PicoGK.BBox3@)
member.summary: This function evaluates the entire voxel field and returns the volume of all voxels in cubic millimeters and the Bounding Box in real world coordinates Note this function is potentially slow, as it needs to traverse the entire voxel field
member.param: Cubic MMs of volume filled with voxels
The real world bounding box of the voxels

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.oCalculateBoundingBox
member.summary: Calculates the bounding box of a voxel field Note: Uses an intermediate mesh generated from the voxels This is the only accurate way of getting a bounding box. OpenVDB keeps spurious surface voxels (distance 0) around which show up in the bounding box, even though the voxel field does not actually contain any volume.
member.returns: Bounding box of the voxels in real world coordinates

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.bIsInside(System.Numerics.Vector3)
member.summary: Returns whether the location specified lies inside the solid domain of the voxel field (are you at or below the surface)
member.returns: True if inside or on the surface, False if outside

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.vecSurfaceNormal(System.Numerics.Vector3@)
member.summary: Returns the normal of the surface found at the specified point. Use after functions like bClosestPointOnSurface or bRayCastToSurface
member.param: The point (on the surface of a voxel field, for which to return the normal
member.returns: The normal vector of the surface at the point

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.bClosestPointOnSurface(System.Numerics.Vector3@,System.Numerics.Vector3@)
member.summary: Returns the closest point from the search point on the surface of the voxel field
member.param: Point on the surface of the voxel field which is closest to the supplied point.
member.returns: True if point is found, false if field is empty

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.vecClosestPointOnSurface(System.Numerics.Vector3@)
member.summary: Returns the closest point from the search point on the surface of the voxel field
member.returns: Point on the surface of the voxel field which is closest to the supplied point.
member.exception: Throws an exception if no point found, which means the voxel field is empty

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.bRayCastToSurface(System.Numerics.Vector3@,System.Numerics.Vector3@,System.Numerics.Vector3@)
member.summary: Casts a ray to the surface of a voxel field and finds the point on the surface where the ray intersects.
member.returns: True, point found. False, no surface intersection found

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.vecRayCastToSurface(System.Numerics.Vector3@,System.Numerics.Vector3@)
member.summary: Casts a ray to the surface of a voxel field and finds the point on the surface where the ray intersects.
member.returns: Point on surface
member.exception: Throws an exception of no intersection with surface found.

Size in z direction in voxels

member.summary: Query the real world origin of a voxel slice, which is also the origin of the actual voxel field in space
member.returns: Real world coordinates of the origin of the slice

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.nSliceCount
member.summary: Return the number of slices in this voxel field
member.returns: Number of slices in the voxel field (equivalent to nZSice)

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.imgAllocateSlice(System.Int32@,PicoGK.Voxels.ESliceAxis)
member.summary: Allocate a grayscale image that can hold a voxel slice
member.param: Number of slices in the specified axis
Axis to use for the slice direction
member.returns: Allocated grayscale image to be used in GetVoxelSlice

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.GetVoxelSlice(System.Int32@,PicoGK.ImageGrayScale@,PicoGK.Voxels.ESliceMode,PicoGK.Voxels.ESliceAxis)
member.summary: Returns a slice of the voxel field along the specified axis. To use it, use GetVoxelDimensions to find out the size of the voxel field in voxel units. Then allocate a new grayscale image to copy the data into, and pass it as a reference. Since GetVoxelDimensions is potentially an "expensive" function, we are putting the burden on you to allocate an image and don't create it for you. You can also re-use the image if you want to save an entire image stack
member.param: Slice to retrieve. 0 is at the origin.
Pre-allocated grayscale image to receive the values

Encoding mode of the image, defaults to signed distance, which is the native narrow band distance encoded in the float image. You can also speciy black/white, which encodes the inside of the field as black pixels, or antialiased, which encodes the outside narrow band in grayscale
Axis to slice along, defaults to Z, but you can also slice along X and Y

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.GetInterpolatedVoxelSlice(System.Single@,PicoGK.ImageGrayScale@,PicoGK.Voxels.ESliceMode)
member.summary: Returns a signed distance-field-encoded slice of the voxel field To use it, use GetVoxelDimensions to find out the size of the voxel field in voxel units. Then allocate a new grayscale image to copy the data into, and pass it as a reference. Since GetVoxelDimensions is potentially an "expensive" function, we are putting the burden on you to allocate an image and don't create it for you. You can also re-use the image if you want to save an entire image stack
member.param: Slice to retrieve. 0 is at the bottom.
Pre-allocated grayscale image to receive the values

Encoding mode of the image, defaults to signed distance, which is the native narrow band distance encoded in the float image. You can also speciy black/white, which encodes the inside of the field as black pixels, or antialiased, which encodes the outside narrow band in grayscale

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.oVectorize(System.Single,System.Boolean,PicoGK.IProgress)
member.summary: Vectorize a Voxels object using Marching Squares. The result is the outline of each voxel slice with outer contours in counter-clockwise direction and inner contours (islands) in clockwise direction. Supports arbitrary layer height (interpolates the slices between discrete Z values)
member.param: By default the origin lies at 0/0 of the bounding box. If you set this parameter to true, the X/Y coordinates start at the actual position in space where the voxel field lies. This can be useful for display purposes.
Optional parameter that allows you to report the progress
member.returns: The returned PolySliceStack object contains the vectorized layers.
member.exception: An exception is thrown if no slices are detected.

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.SaveToCliFile(System.String,System.Single,PicoGK.CliIo.EFormat,System.Boolean,PicoGK.IProgress)
member.summary: Save the voxel field to a .cli file CLI is an quasi industry standard for exchanging layer information with Laser Powder Bed Fusion (LBPF) industrial 3D printers
member.param: Layer height in mm Typical values are 30 micron (0.03f) or 60 micron (0.06f)
If specified, the CLI file uses the position in space in X/Y that the voxel field was in. By default the position of the CLI slices are relative to the voxel field boundaries.

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.voxFromVdbFile(PicoGK.Library,System.String)
member.summary: Create Voxels from a OpenVDB file (.vdb) This function searches for the first compatible field in the .vdb file (OpenVDB supports storing many fields of various types in one file). PicoGK Voxels need an openvdb::GRID_LEVEL_SET field. Incompatible fields and other compatible fields after the first one are ignored. For more sophisticated .vdb file handling, use PicoGK.OpenVdbFile Note: When you save your own VdbFiles with multiple fields in it you cannot rely on the fields being in the same order as you saved them. In other words, if you use PicoGK.OpenVdbFile to save multiple Voxel fields to a file, loading Voxels with voxFromVdbFile will load any one, not necessarily the one you saved first.
member.param: Path and file name of the VDB file
member.returns: A voxel field, based on the .vdb data
member.exception: If file is empty or no compatible field found, an exception is thrown.

member.name: M:PicoGK.Voxels.SaveToVdbFile(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a new .vdb file and saves the voxel field to it
member.param: Path and filename of the file
member.exception: Throws an exception if unable to save

Value to set in the vector field

The threshold of the signed distance field to be used for the definition of "inside" - usually 0.5 is a good value - the surface is at exactly 0 and a value of 1.0 means you are 1 voxel outside from the surface.

Value to set the Scalar Field to if condition is met

The threshold of the signed distance field to be used for the definition of "inside" - usually 0.5 is a good value - the surface is at exactly 0 and a value of 1.0 means you are 1 voxel outside from the surface.

member.name: M:PicoGK.ScalarField.GetVoxelSlice(System.Int32@,PicoGK.ImageGrayScale@)
member.summary: Returns a signed distance-field-encoded slice of the voxel field To use it, use GetVoxelDimensions to find out the size of the voxel field in voxel units. Then allocate a new grayscale image to copy the data into, and pass it as a reference. Since GetVoxelDimensions is potentially an "expensive" function, we are putting the burden on you to allocate an image and don't create it for you. You can also re-use the image if you want to save an entire image stack
member.param: Slice to retrieve. 0 is at the bottom.
Pre-allocated grayscale image to receive the values

member.summary: Return the scalar value at the specified position as as signed distance value. This assumes you stored an signed distance field in the scalar field (for example by constructing it from a voxel field)
member.returns: Distance to the surface - negative values are inside the object positive values are outside the object, values of 0 are exactly on the surface

member.name: M:PicoGK.Mesh.mshFromStlFile(System.String,PicoGK.Mesh.EStlUnit,System.Single,System.Nullable{System.Numerics.Vector3},PicoGK.Library)
member.summary: Loads a mesh from an STL file By default, it tries to find a UNITS= info in the header and uses that to scale the mesh automatically to mm. If none is found mm is assumed You can override this behaviour and explicitly specify a unit To explicitly scale the STL, provide the fPostScale parameter vecPostOffsetMM is applied last, offsetting the mesh
member.param: Path to the file. If not found or not able to open, an exception is thrown
Scale parameter to be applied before offset
Offset parameter to be applied last
member.returns: Returns a valid mesh in any case. If file was invalid, the mesh may be empty.

member.name: M:PicoGK.Mesh.SaveToStlFile(System.String,PicoGK.Mesh.EStlUnit,System.Nullable{System.Numerics.Vector3},System.Single)
member.summary: Saves a Mesh to STL file If eUnit is auto, then, if this mesh was loaded from an STL before the same units as before are being used (stored in public property m_eLoadUnits). If not loaded from an STL, defaults to mm. Internal mesh units are always mm, so if a unit other than mm is used, the mesh coordinates are scaled appropriately (divided by 10 for CM units, for example). Our STL loaders use the UNIT= parameter in the header, so whatever units you use, you will always get an STL with the same dimensions if you load it again using our loader. However many STL importers out there, ignore the UNIT info in the header. You can explicitly offset the mesh before saving (offset in mm) after offset, a scale parameter can be applied. The last operation is always the conversion to the unit
member.param: File path. If not writable, exceptions is thrown
If loaded previously, defaults to original units. MM if not previously loaded. Can be overridden to save to a specifc unit.
Offset applied while still in mm units
Scale applied after offset, while still in mm units

member.name: T:PicoGK.OpenVdbFile
member.summary:
OpenVdbFile handles the creation, loading and saving of openvdb .VDB files. These files are a general container for data generated by the OpenVDB library. As such, they have many data types and functionality that PicoGK doesn't support. PicoGK currently allows for the saving and storing of Voxels, ScalarFields, and VectorFields in VDB files.
The following caveats are important:
1. Make sure that the voxel size of the file (fPicoGKVoxelSizeMM) is the same as the one in your PicoGK.Library instance. The easiest way to create a compatible PicoGK.Library instance is to use the oCreateCompatibleLibraryFor static function.
2. If you store more than one field to a .VDB file, you cannot rely on the first saved field to also be the first to be retrieved. OpenVDB reshuffles the order of the fields. The easiest way around that is to retrieve fields by name, if your .vdb file contains multiple
3. There are many data types that PicoGK currently doesn't support. PicoGK.Voxels are based on the OpenVDB type GRID_LEVEL_SET. Other types, such as GRID_FOG_VOLUME are not supported.
4. If you just want to save/store VDBs from PicoGK, and only have one field per VDB, you can use the helper functions in VoxelsIo which are easier to use.
Workflow for creating .VDB files - Create an empty OpenVdbFile object - Add the fields you want to save to it - Save it to file
Workflow for loading VDB files - Load .VDB file from disk into a OpenVdbFile object - Query the object for the fields stored, retrieving names and types - Get the Voxels from the field with the right name and type

member.name: T:PicoGK.Utils.TempFolder
member.summary:
Creates a temporary folder with an arbitrary filename in the system's default temp directory, which is guaranteed to be writable (we don't check this, but the system should guarantee it) Use the "using" syntax to automatically cleanup after the object runs out of scope. It will clean up all the files inside and then delete the temporary folder. Note: It intentionally doesn't delete any subdirectories you may create. If you create subdirs, please clean them up yourself. We intentionally do not recursively wipe out everything out of an abundance of caution.
Access the temp folder using oFolder.strFolder

member.summary: The Library implements the Dispose pattern, so you can use it with `using`

member.name: M:PicoGK.Library.Go(System.Single,System.Threading.ThreadStart,System.String,System.Boolean,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: This is the one library function that you call to run your code it sets up the PicoGK library, with the specified voxel size and builds the PicoGK environment with viewer, log and other internals The fnTask you pass is called after everything is set up correctly inside of fnTask, you do your processing, displaying it in Library::oTheViewer and logging info with Library::Log()
member.param: If true, the viewer exits when your task is done
The title of your viewer window
A specific lighting environment to load
member.exception: Throws an exception, for a number of scenarios, for example, if the library cannot be found, folders, etc. cannot be created, etc. Always handle the exception to understand what's going on.

member.name: M:PicoGK.Library.bContinueTask(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Checks whether the task started using Go() should continue, and returns true if that's the case or false otherwise. If your task can take a non-trivial amount of time, check this function periodically. If it returns false, exit the task function as soon as possible.
member.param: If true, the bContinueTask function will only take into consideration if the application is about to exit. Any pending EndTask() requests will be ignored.

member.name: M:PicoGK.Library.EndTask
member.summary: Requests the task started by the Go() function to end. Note that it's the responsability of the task to call the bContinueTask() function periodically and to honor these requests.

member.name: M:PicoGK.Library.CancelEndTaskRequest
member.summary: Cancels any pending request to end the task.

member.summary: Returns the library name (from the C++ side)
member.returns: The name of the dynamically loaded C++ library

member.summary: Returns the library version (from the C++ side)
member.returns: The library version of the C++ library

member.name: M:PicoGK.Library.strBuildInfo
member.summary: Returns internal build info, such as build date/time of the C++ library
member.returns: Internal build info of the C++ library

member.name: T:PicoGK.CliIo
member.summary: ASCII CLI (Common Layer Interface) I/O based on https://www.hmilch.net/downloads/cli_format.html#:~:text=CLI%20is%20intended%20as%20a,data%20structure%20of%20the%20machine. Probably doesn't work if each $$ command is not in a separate line (\n) the spec is not clear whether this should be possible The parser could probably be written in a more concise way if it generalized the structure $$ .. / for commands. But this is generally not a well-though-out format.

member.param: Path and filename of the CLI file
Optional date (if empty, current date is used)
Units to be used (in MM), 1000f results in coordinates to be written in meters

Thrown if unsupported features encountered, notably binary is not supported

member.name: T:PicoGK.Shapes.IPath2d
member.summary: Interface to represent a normalized path in 2D space which travels from 0..1

member.name: M:PicoGK.Shapes.IPath2d.vecPtAtT(System.Single)
member.summary: Returns the point at position t (0..1) As t increases monotonically, the point moves along the contour at constant speed with respect to arc length

member.name: P:PicoGK.Shapes.IPath2d.fLength
member.summary: Length of the entire contour

member.name: T:PicoGK.Shapes.IContour2d
member.summary: Interface to represent a normalized closed contour in 2D which travels from 0..1 - Position at 0 and 1 are identical - The contour is centered around the coordinate 0/0 - The contour is in counter-clockwise winding order

member.summary: Helper function to create simple rounded polygons based on the Supershape https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Superformula

member.summary: Length of the entire contour

member.summary: A two dimensional closed contour aligned in a plane in 3D space Note: While a IPath3d can contain random segments in space, a contour needs to have all its points aligned in one plane The point at 0 and 1 are identical (closed contour)

member.name: T:PicoGK.Shapes.Frame3d
member.summary: The Frame3d object stores a local coordinate system, i.e. a rigid transformation from a local coordinate to a world coordinate. The transformation consists of translation (move) and rotation, but not scaling, shearing or other complex transformations.

member.summary: Local frame representing the world coordinate system

member.summary: Create a Frame3d at the specified position with local axes aligned with the specified world Z and X directions. The function constructs a right-handed coordinate system that is orthogonal, even if the directions specified are only approximate.

member.summary: Creates a local coordinate system from approximate Z and X; enforces orthonormality and right-handedness.

member.summary: Creates a Frame3d from a column-vector Matrix4x4. Rows convention: [X; Y; Z; Origin] with translation in last column.

Write only frames that contain defective values (such as NaN, Infinity)

member.name: T:PicoGK.VoxCutViz
member.summary: Visualizes the result of a voxel filed cut along an axis slice. Useful for showing the inside of a voxel field. You can set up two slice planes along the X,Y,Z axes. The class uses two SliceViz objects to show the contents of the signed distance field, and then uses a boolean operation to cut the object along these slices. It then shows the result in the viewer.

member.name: M:PicoGK.VoxCutViz.#ctor(PicoGK.Viewer,PicoGK.Voxels,System.Int32,PicoGK.Voxels.ESliceAxis)
member.summary: Initializes a new VoxCutViz object with the specified Viewer and VoxelField. To use the object, encapsulate it into using VoxCutViz oViz = new (...) this will make the visualization disappear, once it goes out of scope.
member.param: Voxel field to slice and cut
Viewer group to use for the sliced object

member.name: P:PicoGK.VoxCutViz.nSliceCount
member.summary: Number of slices in the voxel field

member.name: M:PicoGK.VoxCutViz.Cut(System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Cut the voxel field along the two normalized values (0 is the first slice 1 is the last)

member.name: M:PicoGK.VoxCutViz.Cut(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Cut the voxel field along the two absolute slice values (0..nSliceCount-1)

member.name: M:PicoGK.VoxCutViz.Dispose
member.summary: Call to stop the visualization (or let the object go out of scope, if you used using)
